insights.title: Setting gain to -100 dB produces silence, not -inf
insights.summary: The core.gain node treats -100 dB as a special silence threshold and clears the output buffer completely at that point. This is by design, but it means you cannot achieve true -inf (zero) digitally through the gain node alone. For pure mathematical zero, use a math node combined with a smoothed_parameter node to avoid clicks.
insights.verify_hint: Check C++ source for the -100 dB threshold shortcut in the gain node processing.

insights.title: Use cable_expr to remap a gain parameter to a sub-range
insights.summary: When you need the gain to respond only to a portion of a knob's travel (e.g. 0 dB to +3 dB for the upper half of a knob), use a `cable_expr` node to apply a custom formula. This requires the network to be compiled. Alternatively, adjusting the skew factor of the connected UI knob can achieve a similar effect without SNEX compilation.
insights.verify_hint: Demonstrate cable_expr remapping gain range in a compiled scriptnode network.

insights.title: Expose compressor gain reduction via DisplayBuffer
insights.summary: To display gain reduction from a scriptnode compressor on the UI, set the compressor's display buffer to 'External', then use `Synth.getDisplayBufferSource()` to fetch a reference and read `buffer.getMagnitude()` inside a timer callback. This is the canonical pattern for all scriptnode modulation output visualisation.
insights.verify_hint: Verify the API: Synth.getDisplayBufferSource(), getDisplayBuffer(index), setRingBufferProperties(), getReadBuffer().

insights.title: Fetch node modulation output without a global cable using DSPNetwork API
insights.summary: It is not necessary to route a global cable out of a scriptnode network just to read a node's parameter or modulation value on the UI side. The DSPNetwork and Node scripting APIs can be used to directly query any node's parameter value from HiseScript, enabling more direct gain-reduction metering without extra wiring.
insights.verify_hint: Check DSPNetwork and Node scripting API methods for reading parameter values at runtime.

insights.title: Faust vbargraph translates to a scriptnode modulation output
insights.summary: When using a Faust node inside scriptnode, any `vbargraph` primitive declared in the Faust code becomes a modulation output of the faust node. This can be connected to a `core.peak` node or wired to a global cable to expose values like gain reduction to the UI layer.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm vbargraph -> modulation output mapping in the Faust integration code.

insights.title: Use RMS mode on dry_wet_mixer for automatic gain matching
insights.summary: The `dry_wet_mixer` node has a dropdown to select RMS mode which applies equal-power (RMS-based) gain compensation automatically. This is the simplest way to achieve a gain-matched dry/wet blend without manually calculating compensation values.
insights.verify_hint: Confirm the RMS dropdown option exists in the dry_wet_mixer node.

insights.title: The Width parameter of core.gain operates on the mid/side signal
insights.summary: The Width parameter does not apply pseudo-stereo or additional processing; it amplifies or attenuates the side (L-R) signal. Setting Width beyond 100% continues to amplify the side channel, which can cause the output to exceed the original level.
insights.verify_hint: Review source code for the Width parameter implementation in the SimpleGain/gain node.
